title: Classic Red Barn Bunk Bed
summary: A bunk bed built to look like a red barn, complete with a peaked roof, a hinged guard-rail "door," and built-in stairs.
description:
This is a bunk bed and a piece of stage scenery at the same time — the two barn-end gables carry the whole structure, and everything else (bed frame, stairs, roof, guard rail) attaches to them. Because the theme depends on getting the gable angle and roofline right, cut and dry-fit both barn ends before you build anything else; they need to be identical mirror images of each other, or the roof won't sit evenly.

The guard rail hinges open like a barn door instead of lifting out, which is a nice detail but means the hinge hardware is doing real structural work every time a kid climbs in — use real bed-rail-rated hinges and hooks, not decorative ones. Paint goes on last and after a full sanding pass; a barn-red build like this lives or dies on the finish looking crisp, not on the joinery being invisible.

tools.note: For the angled barn-gable roof cuts.
The bed frame, stairs, and roofline all depend on the two barn ends standing level with each other.

materials.name: Trim boards
materials.note: Decorative barn door and window trim.

materials.name: Metal strip
materials.note: Supports the roof pieces at the door opening.

materials.name: Bed-rail hinges
materials.note: Rated for structural/safety use — the guard rail hinges open like a door, not a decorative panel.

materials.name: Exterior/interior paint
materials.note: Classic barn red plus a trim color.

steps.title: Build both barn ends as mirror images
steps.body: Assemble both barn-end gable frames. Secure each one together with a 2x4 along the bottom and another at the height you want the bed frame — set that second 2x4 in from the ends by 1-1/2" on each side, which leaves room to attach the rest of the bed frame later. Build the two ends as true mirror images of each other; the roofline and stairs both depend on them matching.

steps.title: Cut the barn roof angles
steps.body: Cut the angled peak on each barn end. Dry-fit the two ends together with a straightedge across both peaks to confirm they match before moving on — a mismatch here throws off every roof board later.

steps.title: Set the middle rail — it becomes the bed frame
steps.body: The middle rail installed in the barn ends does double duty: it's the mounting point the bed frame rails attach to. Confirm its height now, since it's much harder to adjust once the frame is loaded.

steps.title: Assemble the bed frame
steps.body: Attach the bed frame to the middle 2x4 rail set in the previous step — lag screws give the most secure joint here since the frame carries real weight. Attach one 2x6 rail on each side, then run 2x6 slats between them.

steps.title: Build the stairs
steps.body: Build the stairs from two 2x4s secured to each barn end at the bottom, building up with 2x6 treads to the height of the bed frame. Make the stair width 16-1/2" (three 2x6s across).

steps.title: Add the roof
steps.body: Attach the roof boards, leaving an opening over the stairs so there's a way into the bed. Use the metal strip to support the ends of the roof pieces where they meet the door opening, and attach at least one full-length roof piece straight to the bed frame.

steps.title: Hang the guard rail
steps.body: Attach the guard rail along the open side of the bed frame using four bed-rail-rated hinges, plus two hooks at either end to hold it closed. This rail is doing real safety work — don't substitute decorative hardware.

steps.title: Add the decorative barn door and window trim
steps.body: Attach trim boards to the barn-end faces to suggest a barn door and window — this is purely decorative and where the theme comes together visually.

steps.title: Sand and paint
steps.body: Sand the whole bed thoroughly, then paint in classic barn colors — red body, a contrasting trim color. Let each coat cure fully before the bed goes back into use.

images.alt: Kids bunk bed built and painted to look like a red barn, with built-in stairs and a hinged guard rail
